stemHtml: Select the correct definition of human blood pressure:

options.a: Diastolic pressure is the lowest measurable pressure exerted by the heart on the vein walls, when the blood flows towards the heart to be re-oxygenated.
options.b: Systolic pressure is generated by the contractions of the heart, when it pumps blood towards the periphery of the body, and it is the highest pressure value.
options.c: Diastolic pressure is the highest blood pressure value, and it is measured when the heart contracts to force blood back from the periphery of the body.
options.d: The lowest blood pressure values are measured between contractions of the heart on the artery walls, and are called systolic pressure.
